Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map
Family: coding

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

FINAL_ANSWER: E = 2500 - N_min, where N_min is the minimum number of cells required to embed the adjacency graph of the colored city map.
JUSTIFICATION: To maximize the number of empty cells (E) in a fixed 50x50 grid (total 2500 cells), one must minimize the number of occupied cells (N). The occupied cells must satisfy all color-adjacency and connectivity constraints, which is equivalent to finding the minimum embedding of the city's topological graph into the grid.
